extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads caught CLARKE Simon and DE MARCHI Alessandro in final 200m after breakaway hesitated, completing grand slam of Grand Tour stage wins
tactical_patterns: CLARKE and DE MARCHI lost 33-second advantage with 3km remaining by waiting and watching instead of committing early
THOMAS Geraint needed team-assisted chase through cars after mechanical, revealing GC vulnerability on shorter transitional stages
ROGLIČ Primož mechanical coinciding with THOMAS issue prevented organized peloton chase, gifting breakaway extended survival window

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: demonstrated superior timing judgment by launching sprint at 200m while experienced breakaway riders hesitated
rider_signals.CLARKE Simon: negative: tactical indecision at 36 years old cost near-certain stage win when holding 33 seconds at 3km
rider_signals.DE MARCHI Alessandro: negative: former Maglia Rosa holder failed to force tempo in two-man break despite stronger climbing legs
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: second place extends Maglia Ciclamino lead while avoiding energy expenditure for stage win
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: third place signals emerging sprint form after previously quiet Giro
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: dropped on first categorized climb confirms knee injury from stage 5 crash limiting sprint participation
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: required full team escort after mechanical demonstrates GC defense fragility on disorganized stages
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: mechanical at critical moment prevented chase organization but regained contact without time loss
